(born June 25, 1986[[2]](#cite_note-hawkeye-1-2)) is an American former professional [football](/wiki/American_football "American football") player who was a [cornerback](/wiki/Cornerback "Cornerback") in the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL). He was selected by the [St. Louis Rams](/wiki/St._Louis_Rams "St. Louis Rams") of the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL) in the third round of the [2009 NFL draft](/wiki/2009_NFL_draft "2009 NFL draft"). He played [college football](/wiki/College_football "College football") for the [Iowa Hawkeyes](/wiki/Iowa_Hawkeyes_football "Iowa Hawkeyes football").

["Bradley Fletcher, CB for the St. Louis Rams"](http://www.nfl.com/player/bradleyfletcher/80636/profile). *Player profile at [NFL.com](/wiki/NFL.com "NFL.com")*. [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League"). Retrieved January 13, 2012.

**Bradley Fletcher** (born June 25, 1986[[2]](#cite_note-hawkeye-1-2)) is an American former professional [football](/wiki/American_football "American football") player who was a [cornerback](/wiki/Cornerback "Cornerback") in the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL). He was selected by the [St. Louis Rams](/wiki/St._Louis_Rams "St. Louis Rams") of the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL) in the third round of the [2009 NFL draft](/wiki/2009_NFL_draft "2009 NFL draft"). He played [college football](/wiki/College_football "College football") for the [Iowa Hawkeyes](/wiki/Iowa_Hawkeyes_football "Iowa Hawkeyes football").

Fletcher also played for the [Philadelphia Eagles](/wiki/Philadelphia_Eagles "Philadelphia Eagles") and [New England Patriots](/wiki/New_England_Patriots "New England Patriots").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bradley_Fletcher&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

At [Liberty High School](/wiki/Liberty_High_School_(Ohio) "Liberty High School (Ohio)"), in Youngstown, Ohio, Fletcher was First-team All-Conference, All-District, and All-State as a senior, where he had 74 tackles, 43 solo tackles, two interceptions and six pass break-ups on defense and on offense, as a running back, rushed 143 times for 815 yards 12 touchdowns as a senior. As a junior, he was named First-team All-Conference and honorable mention All-State. He recorded 207 tackles, 81 solo tackles, 13 pass break-ups and four interceptions in prep career along with 17 receptions for 314 yards. Fletcher was also an All-State basketball player.

## College career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bradley_Fletcher&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: College career")]

As a senior at Iowa in 2008 Fletcher recorded 60 tackles, including 42 solo stops and 18 assists and led team with 10 pass break-ups while intercepting three passes. Fletcher redshirted as a freshman in 2004 and saw action as a backup in 2005, recording one tackle in the ten games he played. He was again a backup in 2006 playing in 13 games with 4 starts, recording 38 tackles (24 solo) and a forced fumble. As a junior in 2007 he played the same role as 2006, a backup cornerback. In 2007, he played in 11 games, starting four, recording 52 tackles (30 solo) and intercepted two passes, forced a fumble and recovered one as well while breaking up three passes.

## Professional career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bradley_Fletcher&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Professional career")]

### St. Louis Rams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bradley_Fletcher&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: St. Louis Rams")]

Fletcher was selected by the Rams in the third round of the [2009 NFL draft](/wiki/2009_NFL_draft "2009 NFL draft") with the 66th overall pick.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Philadelphia Eagles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bradley_Fletcher&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Philadelphia Eagles")]

Fletcher signed with the [Philadelphia Eagles](/wiki/Philadelphia_Eagles "Philadelphia Eagles") on March 12, 2013.[[6]](#cite_note-6) In 2014, he allowed the most yards in the NFL (1,072) and the second most touchdowns allowed (9).[[7]](#cite_note-7) After 2 years, the Eagles decided not to re-sign Fletcher and he left in free agency.

### New England Patriots

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bradley_Fletcher&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: New England Patriots")]

Fletcher signed with the [New England Patriots](/wiki/New_England_Patriots "New England Patriots") on March 18, 2015.[[8]](#cite_note-8) He played in two games with New England and was released on October 10, 2015.
